pattern: Over the prefix sums, a monotonic deque keeps candidate left endpoints in increasing order so each right endpoint can pop satisfied and dominated starts in amortized constant time.
hints: Rewrite a subarray sum as the difference of two prefix sums; you want the closest earlier prefix that is small enough.
Because negatives exist a plain sliding window fails, but a deque of prefix indices kept increasing in value lets you both answer and prune greedily.

Compute prefix sums. Maintain a deque of indices with increasing prefix values: for each j pop from the front while prefix[j] minus the front prefix is at least k (recording the length), then pop from the back any index whose prefix is not less than prefix[j] before pushing j.

edge_inputs.description: first example: single element exactly meets k
second example: total sum falls short of k
third example: a negative in the middle forces the full span
single element below k has no valid subarray
a later short run beats an early long one
leading negatives must be skipped, not extended into
all negatives can never reach a positive k
best window sits in the middle after a dip

stress.description: moderate arrays with negatives over a small range so many windows compete
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, size)
    nums = [rng.randint(-20, 30) for _ in range(n)]
    k = rng.randint(1, 200)
    return [nums, k]
stress.note: size kept modest so the O(n^2) brute oracle stays fast
